providers.verification: Restart or reload the Agent, then verify the declared MCP or Skill is discoverable.
providers.notes: Do not create a new canonical skill directory inside this template; use the provider's supported location. Google Workspace access uses the standalone gws terminal command and does not require provider configuration.

providers.verification: Reload Claude Code and run the Skill's minimum verification case.
providers.notes: Google Workspace access uses the standalone gws terminal command and does not require provider configuration.

providers.verification: Restart OpenCode and run the MCP initialize or minimum Skill case. Verify gws separately through its terminal command when selected.
Use the MCP Server Hub or provider settings, then run a non-destructive tool call.
providers.notes: The first release supplies guided manual configuration rather than claiming unattended installation. Google Workspace access uses the standalone gws terminal command.
